questions.question: Define trigonometry and explain its basic idea.
questions.answer: Trigonometry is a branch of mathematics that deals with the study of relationships between the sides and angles of a right-angled triangle. It helps in finding unknown sides or angles when some information is given.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by a right-angled triangle.
questions.answer: A right-angled triangle is a triangle in which one of the angles is equal to 90°. The side opposite the right angle is called the hypotenuse.

questions.question: Define hypotenuse, opposite side and adjacent side with respect to an angle.
questions.answer: In a right triangle, the hypotenuse is the side opposite the right angle. With respect to a given acute angle, the side opposite to it is called the opposite side and the side next to it (excluding hypotenuse) is called the adjacent side.

questions.question: Define sin A, cos A and tan A.
questions.answer: Sin A is the ratio of opposite side to hypotenuse, cos A is the ratio of adjacent side to hypotenuse and tan A is the ratio of opposite side to adjacent side.

questions.question: Explain the six trigonometric ratios.
questions.answer: The six trigonometric ratios are sin A, cos A, tan A, cosec A, sec A and cot A. The last three are reciprocals of the first three.

questions.question: Find the value of sin 30° and explain.
questions.answer: The value of sin 30° is 1/2, obtained using a right-angled triangle.

questions.question: Find the value of cos 60° and explain.
questions.answer: The value of cos 60° is 1/2.

questions.question: Find the value of tan 45°.
questions.answer: The value of tan 45° is 1.

questions.question: Explain why sin 0° = 0.
questions.answer: At 0°, the opposite side becomes zero, hence sin 0° = 0.

questions.question: Explain why cos 90° = 0.
questions.answer: At 90°, the adjacent side becomes zero, hence cos 90° = 0.

questions.question: State and explain the identity sin²A + cos²A = 1.
questions.answer: This identity is derived using Pythagoras theorem in a right triangle.

questions.question: Explain why tan 90° is not defined.
questions.answer: At 90°, the adjacent side becomes zero, making the ratio undefined.

questions.question: Explain why cot 0° is not defined.
questions.answer: At 0°, the opposite side becomes zero, hence cot 0° is undefined.

questions.question: Explain the concept of standard angles.
questions.answer: Standard angles are angles like 0°, 30°, 45°, 60° and 90° whose trigonometric values are fixed.

questions.question: Explain why trigonometric ratios depend only on angles.
questions.answer: Trigonometric ratios are the same for all similar triangles and hence depend only on angles.

questions.question: Explain the reciprocal trigonometric ratios.
questions.answer: Cosec, sec and cot are reciprocals of sin, cos and tan respectively.

questions.question: Find the value of sec 0°.
Find the value of cosec 90°.
questions.answer: cosec 90° = 1.

questions.question: Explain the use of trigonometry in daily life.
questions.answer: Trigonometry is used in height and distance problems, astronomy, navigation and engineering.

questions.question: Explain Pythagoras theorem.
questions.answer: In a right triangle, the square of hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the other two sides.

questions.question: Explain why tan A = sin A / cos A.
questions.answer: By definition, tan A = opposite/adjacent = (opposite/hypotenuse)/(adjacent/hypotenuse).

questions.question: Explain the meaning of an acute angle.
questions.answer: An acute angle is an angle less than 90°.

questions.question: Explain why sin 90° = 1.
questions.answer: At 90°, the opposite side equals the hypotenuse.

questions.question: Explain why cos 0° = 1.
questions.answer: At 0°, the adjacent side equals the hypotenuse.

questions.question: Explain the importance of trigonometric identities.
questions.answer: They help in simplifying expressions and solving trigonometric problems.
